commands.review_summary:
This PR deletes DeepSeek V4 `payload.thinking` from the shared OpenAI-compatible wrapper while keeping `reasoning_effort` for enabled thinking and adding payload-shaping regression tests.

Reproducibility: unclear. for the reported direct-provider 400 because the PR includes only a claim, not attached logs or live output. The patch-introduced disabled-mode regression is source-reproducible from the diff, current docs/tests, and DeepSeek's documented default-enabled toggle.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor real behavior proof and a maintainer repair decision for direct DeepSeek versus Foundry/proxy payload semantics before merge.

target.title: fix(deepseek): stop sending unsupported thinking payload
target.body:
## Summary
- stop sending `payload.thinking` for DeepSeek v4 OpenAI-compatible requests
- keep `reasoning_effort` mapping when thinking is enabled
- strip DeepSeek reasoning fields/content when thinking is disabled
- add regression coverage for enabled and disabled thinking payload shaping

## Why
The previous PR focused on Foundry-hosted DeepSeek behavior, but the actual bug sits in the shared DeepSeek v4 OpenAI-compatible thinking wrapper used by the direct `deepseek` provider path as well.

DeepSeek direct rejects the injected field with:
- `400 Unrecognized request argument supplied: thinking`

## Verification
- verified local installed runtime fix for `microsoft-foundry/deepseek-v4-pro` with `--thinking off|low|high`
- traced direct provider path:
  - `extensions/deepseek/index.ts`
  - `extensions/deepseek/stream.ts`
  - `src/plugin-sdk/provider-stream-shared.ts`
- added regression tests for request shaping in `src/plugin-sdk/provider-stream-shared.test.ts`

## Follow-up to prior PR
This supersedes the earlier narrower framing in #85683. That PR should be closed in favor of this one because the proper fix point is the shared DeepSeek v4 wrapper.
target.author: silvesterxm
